rationale: In the conceptual framework, the cited study is used specifically to justify and define peer-to-peer brand interactions as earned touchpoints, rather than being only a background mention. That earned-touchpoint distinction is central to how the citing paper builds its social-oriented app design construct and related hypotheses about firm value effects. While the citing paper does not reuse the cited paper's model or data, it relies on it in a meaningful conceptual framing role.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Stephen and Galak (2012) as a conceptual foundation for classifying social peer-to-peer brand interactions as earned touchpoints. This foundation supports the citing paper's theorization of social-oriented app features and their moderating impact on app announcement value effects.
